# STL fixture surfaces

Tiny ASCII STL surfaces for the STL → snappyHexMesh pathway tests (spec #58,
harvested for ticket #59). Generated by `gen_stl.py` in this directory —
axis-aligned cubes with programmatically wound outward normals (orientation
asserted per facet), so the geometry checks see deliberate, known defects and
nothing else:

| File | Facets | Deliberate property |
|---|---|---|
| `watertight_cube.stl` | 12 | closed 1 m cube at the origin — the healthy case |
| `open_box.stl` | 10 | the same cube missing its +z face — open edges |
| `two_shells.stl` | 24 | two disjoint 1 m cubes in one solid — 2 unconnected parts |
| `cube_mm.stl` | 12 | 1000-unit cube — a 1 m part exported in millimetres |

Regenerate with `python3 gen_stl.py` (writes into the working directory).
The matching surfaceCheck outputs live in `tests/fixtures/surfacecheck/`.
